title: DMV
description: John's flagship original IP. AJ is a combat-trained meter maid operating in a cyberpunk dystopia where a 6x3 metre rectangle of road surface is the most valuable real estate imaginable. Recurring characters, multiple art styles, comic strips. The centrepiece of the archive.

title: The Streamers
description: An original IP: magical girls each representing a branch of the U.S. armed forces. Navy Girl (Mayflower Shipman), Air Force Girl (Ariel McFly), Coast Guard Girl. Includes a dedicated Inktober 2018 series. Distinct enough from everything else to stand completely alone.

title: Star Trek
description: The full Inktober 2016 run — chibi and super-deformed Trek ships, Klingon, Romulan, Dominion, Federation. Deep fandom with strong opinions about canon. Too large and too considered to tuck under a generic fan art label.

title: Evil Diva
description: Fan art for the Angry Viking Press indie comic about a demon girl who wants to be an angel. Diva, Loki, Virgil — a recurring cast in a demonic high school where the supernatural is purely administrative.

title: Princeless
description: Fan work for Jeremy Whitley's series. Adrienne, Sparky the dragon, Bedelia. Consistent enough across multiple pieces to earn its own corner of the archive.

title: Anime Fan Art
description: El Hazard, Gundam Girls, Bubblegum Crisis, Robotech, Dirty Pair, Cowboy Bebop, Silent Möbius. Classic catalogue, obvious affection. One bucket that earns its place.

title: Chiroines
description: John's ongoing chibi heroine series — Batgirl, Storm, Starbuck, Harley Quinn, Baroness, Mikasa. Sits between fan art and original concept. The format is his own; the subjects are borrowed with obvious love.

title: Empowered & Miscellaneous
description: Guest work on Adam Warren's Empowered: Animal Style, convention sketches, and one-offs that don't belong anywhere else but belong here.
